mechanism_narrative: A sustained year-over-year increase in nonfarm payrolls indicates ongoing labor market expansion that supports consumer spending and corporate revenue expectations, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC for the PAYEMS trend factor falls below 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window, or the positive return rate in the top quintile of payroll changes is not significantly above the unconditional baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A rapid rise in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.3 percentage points over a trailing three-month window signals labor market deterioration that precedes earnings downgrades and risk-off equity repositioning, generating negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward 60-day returns are positive in more than 50 percent of triggered instances, or the Rank IC of the UNRATE change signal is positive over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: The simultaneous combination of rising federal funds rates and accelerating consumer price inflation tightens financial conditions in a non-linear fashion, eroding real corporate earnings and equity valuations, which produces negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The interaction term fails to add incremental explanatory power beyond main effects in a 3-year rolling regression (F-test p > 0.05), or average 20-day returns in the joint high-FEDFUNDS high-CPIAUCSL regime are not significantly negative.

mechanism_narrative: During late-cycle expansions, a month-over-month contraction in industrial production triggers negative earnings revision momentum and inventory destocking fears, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: During identified late-cycle expansions, INDPRO contraction instances produce positive 60-day returns in more than 50 percent of occurrences, or the signal Rank IC is positive over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: An unexpected surge in retail and food services sales indicates stronger-than-anticipated nominal consumer demand, lifting near-term revenue surprises and driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive 5-day return rate following RSAFS surge trigger events falls below 55 percent over any 2-year rolling window, or the Rank IC of the signal drops below 0.03.

mechanism_narrative: Quarter-over-quarter acceleration in real GDP growth signals broad-based aggregate demand expansion that reduces recession probability and supports equity risk premiums, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 60-day returns are negative in more than 40 percent of triggered instances during post-recession recovery windows, or the Rank IC of GDPC1 acceleration falls below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Elevated federal funds rates above a neutral threshold increase the cost of corporate borrowing and constrain share buybacks, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward 20-day returns are positive in more than 50 percent of months when FEDFUNDS exceeds 3.0 percent, or the Rank IC of the rate level signal is positive over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: The joint acceleration of retail sales and housing starts captures broad-based consumer and residential investment strength that single-series measures miss, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The interaction of RSAFS and HOUST fails to outperform the best single-series model in a 3-year rolling regression (F-test p > 0.05), or the joint signal Rank IC falls below 0.03.
